title: calceolaria integrifolia subvar. rugosa
intro: Calceolaria integrifolia subvar. rugosa, also known as the slipper flower, is a plant species that belongs to the Calceolaria genus, characterized by its unique, slipper-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Calceolaria integrifolia subvar. rugosa include its ability to promote digestion, reduce fever, and act as a natural remedy for various skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and flowers can be used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures or salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
